value.Title: EDP Renewables Signs Agreement with Meta for 250 MW of Solar Energy in Arkansas

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJM-35H3-S02V-B1PN-00000-00EDP Renewables Signs Agreement with Meta for 250 MW of Solar Energy in Arkansas2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:17ZLexisNexisEDP Renewables Signs Agreement with Meta for 250 MW of Solar Energy in Arkansas

Meta and EDP Renewables North America (EDPRNA) today announced a longterm power purchase agreement (PPA) for Cypress Knee Solar, a 250megawatt (MW) solar energy project to be built in Arkansas. The agreement supports Meta's efforts to add new generation to the grid as it continues to match 100% of its annual electricity use with new clean and renewable energy.

Cypress Knee Solar represents Meta's third new energy agreement with EDPRNA; the total procured energy between the two companies has now reached 545 MW, deepening a multiyear collaboration that continues to expand new energy infrastructure across the United States.

"Cypress Knee Solar and our broader portfolio of projects with Meta are helping power a reliable, modern U.S. electric gridthe backbone of American innovation and long-term economic growth," said Sandhya Ganapathy, CEO of EDPR NA. "These investments strengthen local communities, create durable economic value, and ensure that progress is built on a resilient, sustainable foundation. EDPR NA is proud to work with Meta as we deliver domestic power and advanced infrastructure that reinforce American energy independence and expand economic opportunity nationwide."

"Through our partnership with EDPR, Cypress Knee Solar will bring new generation to the Arkansas grid, creating local jobs and delivering economic benefits to the community. We're proud to expand our collaboration with EDPR," said Amanda Yang, Head of Clean and Renewable Energy, Meta.

Cypress Knee Solar will deliver longlasting economic value to communities in Arkansas. Once operational, the project will generate approximately $25 million new revenue for Chicot County, helping fund essential public services and infrastructure improvements. During construction, the project is expected to support several hundred jobs. Cypress Knee is expected to be completed by 2028.

In furtherance of its commitment to meaningful community partnership and collaboration, EDPR NA has donated more than $77,000 to local institutions and community-based initiatives in Chicot County, including the restoration of the Lakeside High School greenhouse, resources and supplies for the Lakeside Robotics team, and funding for the Lake Village food pantry.

value.Title: EDP and Meta sign PPA for 250 MW solar project in Arkansas

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHJ-YTX3-RWJJ-42NV-00000-00EDP and Meta sign PPA for 250 MW solar project in Arkansas2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:17ZLexisNexisEDP and Meta sign PPA for 250 MW solar project in ArkansasNew Delhi


May 07 --

EDP, through EDP Renewables North America, has signed a long-term power purchase agreement (PPA) with Meta for the 250 MW Cypress Knee Solar project in Arkansas. The project is scheduled for completion by 2028. The agreement marks Meta’s third clean energy partnership with EDPR NA, taking the companies’ total contracted renewable energy portfolio to 545 MW in US. Furthermore, the project will support Meta’s goal of matching 100 per cent of its annual electricity consumption with renewable energy while adding new generation capacity to the grid.



In April 2026, Zelestra and EDP signed a power purchase agreement (PPA) to hybridise an operational solar plant with battery storage. As per the agreement, Zelestra will add a 160 MWh BESS to the 50 MW Pizarroso solar plant in Cáceres. The solar plant has been operational since 2023. EDP, the current offtaker for the solar plant, will use the battery storage to improve energy management and enhance system flexibility. 

In January 2026, EDP Renewables North America LLC commenced commercial operations at the Riverstart Solar IV project in Indiana. Located in Randolph county, the project has an installed capacity of 150 MW. It is expected to generate electricity equivalent to the annual consumption of more than 28,800 homes and businesses in the state. The project has delivered an estimated $88.6 million in local and regional economic impact.

value.Title: Meta, EDPR NA sign PPA for 250MW Cypress Knee solar project

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-0C53-RS65-K1K5-00000-00Meta, EDPR NA sign PPA for 250MW Cypress Knee solar project2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:17ZLexisNexisMeta, EDPR NA sign PPA for 250MW Cypress Knee solar project

The Cypress Knee Solar project is the third energy agreement between Meta and EDPR NA.

EDP Renewables North America (EDPR NA) has signed a long-term power purchase agreement (PPA) with Meta for the 250MW Cypress Knee Solar project to be constructed in Arkansas, US.

The agreement is part of Metas initiative to align its annual electricity consumption entirely with renewable sources by integrating new energy generation into the grid.

The Cypress Knee Solar project is the third energy agreement between Meta and EDPR NA, bringing their joint energy procurement to 545MW.

Meta clean and renewable energy head Amanda Yang said: Through our partnership with EDPR, Cypress Knee Solar will bring new generation to the Arkansas grid, creating local jobs and delivering economic benefits to the community. We are proud to expand our collaboration with EDPR.

The ongoing collaboration is focused on expanding renewable energy infrastructure throughout the US.

Once operational, the Cypress Knee Solar project is expected to generate approximately $25m in revenue for Chicot County, contributing to public services and infrastructure improvements.

The construction phase is set to create several hundred jobs.

Completion of the solar farm is targeted for 2028.

EDPR NA CEO Sandhya Ganapathy said: Cypress Knee Solar and our broader portfolio of projects with Meta are helping power a reliable, modern US electric grid the backbone of American innovation and long-term economic growth.

These investments strengthen local communities, create durable economic value and ensure that progress is built on a resilient, sustainable foundation.

EDPRNA is proud to work with Meta as we deliver domestic power and advanced infrastructure that reinforce American energy independence and expand economic opportunity nationwide.

In January this year, EDPR NA commenced commercial operations at Riverstart Solar IV, a 150MW clean energy project in Randolph County, Indiana, US.

Riverstart Solar IV will generate enough electricity each year to power more than 28,800 local homes and businesses, strengthening Indianas grid reliability and supporting regional economic growth.

value.Title: Meta Signs 250 MW U.S. Renewables Deal with EDP Renewables
value.Overview: ESG Today, RENEWABLE ENERGY NEWS & US NEWS, 284words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHT-T9F3-SJ7K-G3VV-00000-00Meta Signs 250 MW U.S. Renewables Deal with EDP Renewables2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:17ZLexisNexisRENEWABLE ENERGY NEWS & US NEWSMeta Signs 250 MW U.S. Renewables Deal with EDP RenewablesMark Segal

Link to Image

Renewable energy producer (EDPR) EDP Renewables North America (EDPRNA) and Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp owner Meta announced a new long-term power purchase agreement (PPA) for a new 250-megawatt (MW) solar energy project, Cypress Knee Solar, to be built in Arkansas.

The new agreement marks Meta's third with EDPR NA, and nearly doubles the total energy procured between the companies to 545 MW. Meta ranked as the largest corporate clean energy offtaker globally in 2025, according to a recent report by BloombergNEF, contracting 10.24 GW in the year. The company has set targets to reach net-zero emissions across its value chain by 2030, and to continue to match 100% of the electricity used in its data centers and offices with renewable energy.

Amanda Yang, Head of Clean and Renewable Energy, Meta, said:

"Through our partnership with EDPR, Cypress Knee Solar will bring new generation to the Arkansas grid, creating local jobs and delivering economic benefits to the community. We're proud to expand our collaboration with EDPR."

The new project is located close to the city ofLake Village, Arkansas, is expected to include approximately $400 million of capital investment, and to generate energy equivalent to the average consumption of more than 43,400 homes, with completion anticipated by 2028.

Sandhya Ganapathy, CEO of EDPR NA, said:

"Cypress Knee Solar and our broader portfolio of projects with Meta are helping power a reliable, modern U.S. electric grid-the backbone of American innovation and long-term economic growth. These investments strengthen local communities, create durable economic value, and ensure that progress is built on a resilient, sustainable foundation.

value.Title: Meta, EDPR NA sign PPA for 250MW Cypress Knee solar project
value.Overview: MarketLine NewsWire (Formerly Datamonitor), ENERGY & UTILITIES; Technology & Telecommunications, 346words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-R9J3-S2S8-F431-00000-00Meta, EDPR NA sign PPA for 250MW Cypress Knee solar project2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:17ZLexisNexisENERGY & UTILITIESTechnology & TelecommunicationsMeta, EDPR NA sign PPA for 250MW Cypress Knee solar project

The Cypress Knee Solar project is the third energy agreement between Meta and EDPR NA.

EDP Renewables North America (EDPR NA) has signed a long-term power purchase agreement (PPA) with Meta for the 250MW Cypress Knee Solar project to be constructed in Arkansas, US. The agreement is part of Meta's initiative to align its annual electricity consumption entirely with renewable sources by integrating new energy generation into the grid. The Cypress Knee Solar project is the third energy agreement between Meta and EDPRNA, bringing their joint energy procurement to 545MW.

Meta clean and renewable energy head Amanda Yang said: "Through our partnership with EDPR, Cypress Knee Solar will bring new generation to the Arkansas grid, creating local jobs and delivering economic benefits to the community. We are proud to expand our collaboration with EDPR." The ongoing collaboration is focused on expanding renewable energy infrastructure throughout the US. Once operational, the Cypress Knee Solar project is expected to generate approximately $25m in revenue for Chicot County, contributing to public services and infrastructure improvements. The construction phase is set to create several hundred jobs. Completion of the solar farm is targeted for 2028. EDPR NA CEO Sandhya Ganapathy said: "Cypress Knee Solar and our broader portfolio of projects with Meta are helping power a reliable, modern US electric grid - the backbone of American innovation and long-term economic growth. "These investments strengthen local communities, create durable economic value and ensure that progress is built on a resilient, sustainable foundation. "EDPR NA is proud to work with Meta as we deliver domestic power and advanced infrastructure that reinforce American energy independence and expand economic opportunity nationwide." In January this year, EDPR NA commenced commercial operations at Riverstart Solar IV, a 150MW clean energy project in Randolph County, Indiana, US. Riverstart Solar IV will generate enough electricity each year to power more than 28,800 local homes and businesses, strengthening Indiana's grid reliability and supporting regional economic growth.

value.Title: Research from Fayetteville Provides New Study Findings on Agriculture (The U.S. Parboiled Rice Production: Processing Innovations, Market Trends, and Circular Economy Pathways)

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-T3H3-S7MW-X3V7-00000-00Research from Fayetteville Provides New Study Findings on Agriculture (The U.S. Parboiled Rice Production: Processing Innovations, Market Trends, and Circular Economy Pathways)2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:17ZLexisNexisAGRICULTUREResearch from Fayetteville Provides New Study Findings on Agriculture (The U.S. Parboiled Rice Production: Processing Innovations, Market Trends, and Circular Economy Pathways)


2026 MAY 07 (NewsRx) -- By a News Reporter-Staff News Editor at Economics Daily Report -- New study results on agriculture have been published. According to news originating from Fayetteville, Arkansas, by NewsRx editors, the research stated, "Parboiling enhances the nutritional, structural, and economic value of rice, yet its adoption in the United States remains limited despite rising domestic and export demand."


Our news reporters obtained a quote from the research from University of Arkansas System Division of Agriculture: "This review summarizes key stages of the parboiling process and their effects on milling yield, grain integrity, nutrient retention, and glycemic response. It outlines major industry challenges, including high energy and water use, uneven heating and drying, handling of defective kernels, limited automation in smaller mills, labor shortages, and emerging climate-related risks. Advances such as vacuum soaking, infrared and microwave-assisted drying, smart sensors, and AI-driven control systems show strong potential to improve efficiency and product quality. Circular-economy strategies, including biomass energy recovery, water reuse, and by-product valorization, offer additional sustainability gains."
According to the news editors, the research concluded: "Continued research, modernization, and policy support are critical to strengthen competitiveness and positioning of the U.S. parboiled rice sector for a more resilient and sustainable future."
For more information on this research see: The U.S. Parboiled Rice Production: Processing Innovations, Market Trends, and Circular Economy Pathways. AgriEngineering, 2026,8(4):136. The publisher for AgriEngineering is MDPI AG.
A free version of this journal article is available at https://doi.org/10.3390/agriengineering8040136.
Our news journalists report that more information may be obtained by contacting Kaushik Luthra, Department of Biological and Agricultural Engineering, University of Arkansas System Division of Agriculture, Fayetteville, AR 72701, United States. Additional authors for this research include Abhay Markande, Josiah Ojeniran, Griffiths Atungulu, Kuldeep Yadav.
Keywords for this news article include: University of Arkansas System Division of Agriculture, Fayetteville, Arkansas, United States, North and Central America, Agriculture, Economies.
Our reports deliver fact-based news of research and discoveries from around the world. Copyright 2026, NewsRx LLC

value.Title: -EDP Renewables Signs Agreement with Meta for 250 MW of Solar Energy in Arkansas

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-13R3-SJJT-J3S3-00000-00-EDP Renewables Signs Agreement with Meta for 250 MW of Solar Energy in Arkansas2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:17ZLexisNexis-EDP Renewables Signs Agreement with Meta for 250 MW of Solar Energy in Arkansas

May 5, 2026

Release date- 04052026 - Total procured clean energy between Meta and EDPRNA has now reached 545 MW

LAKE VILLAGE, Ark. - Meta and EDP Renewables North America (EDPRNA) today announced a long-term power purchase agreement (PPA) for Cypress Knee Solar, a 250-megawatt (MW) solar energy project to be built in Arkansas. The agreement supports Meta's efforts to add new generation to the grid as it continues to match 100% of its annual electricity use with new clean and renewable energy.



Cypress Knee Solar represents Meta's third new energy agreement with EDPRNA; the total procured energy between the two companies has now reached 545 MW, deepening a multi-year collaboration that continues to expand new energy infrastructure across the United States.

'Cypress Knee Solar and our broader portfolio of projects with Meta are helping power a reliable, modern U.S. electric grid-the backbone of American innovation and long-term economic growth,' said Sandhya Ganapathy, CEO of EDPRNA. 'These investments strengthen local communities, create durable economic value, and ensure that progress is built on a resilient, sustainable foundation. EDPR NA is proud to work with Meta as we deliver domestic power and advanced infrastructure that reinforce American energy independence and expand economic opportunity nationwide.'

'Through our partnership with EDPR, Cypress Knee Solar will bring new generation to the Arkansas grid, creating local jobs and delivering economic benefits to the community. We're proud to expand our collaboration with EDPR,' said Amanda Yang, Head of Clean and Renewable Energy, Meta.

Cypress Knee Solar will deliver long-lasting economic value to communities in Arkansas. Once operational, the project will generate approximately $ 25 million new revenue for Chicot County, helping fund essential public services and infrastructure improvements. During construction, the project is expected to support several hundred jobs. Cypress Knee is expected to be completed by 2028.

In furtherance of its commitment to meaningful community partnership and collaboration, EDPR NA has donated more than $ 77,000 to local institutions and community-based initiatives in Chicot County, including the restoration of the Lakeside High School greenhouse, resources and supplies for the Lakeside Robotics team, and funding for the Lake Village food pantry.

About EDP Renewables North America

EDP Renewables North America LLC, its affiliates, and its subsidiaries develop, construct, own, and operate wind farms, solar parks, and energy storage systems throughout North America. Headquartered in Houston, Texas, with 61 wind farms, 29 solar parks, four energy storage sites, and eight regional offices across North America, EDPR NA has developed more than 12,900 megawatts (MW) and operates more than 12,300 MW of onshore utility-scale renewable energy projects. With more than 1,000 employees, EDPR NA's highly qualified team has a proven capacity to execute projects across the continent.

For more information, visit www.edprnorthamerica.com and follow us on LinkedIn.

value.Title: -EDP Renewables Signs Agreement with Meta for 250 MW of Solar Energy in Arkansas

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-13R3-SJJT-J359-00000-00-EDP Renewables Signs Agreement with Meta for 250 MW of Solar Energy in Arkansas2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:17ZLexisNexis-EDP Renewables Signs Agreement with Meta for 250 MW of Solar Energy in Arkansas

Total procured clean energy between Meta and EDPRNA has now reached 545 MW

LAKE VILLAGE, Ark. - Meta and EDP Renewables North America (EDPRNA) today announced a long-term power purchase agreement (PPA) for Cypress Knee Solar, a 250-megawatt (MW) solar energy project to be built in Arkansas. The agreement supports Meta's efforts to add new generation to the grid as it continues to match 100% of its annual electricity use with new clean and renewable energy.



Cypress Knee Solar represents Meta's third new energy agreement with EDPRNA; the total procured energy between the two companies has now reached 545 MW, deepening a multi-year collaboration that continues to expand new energy infrastructure across the United States.

'Cypress Knee Solar and our broader portfolio of projects with Meta are helping power a reliable, modern U.S. electric grid-the backbone of American innovation and long-term economic growth,' said Sandhya Ganapathy, CEO of EDPRNA. 'These investments strengthen local communities, create durable economic value, and ensure that progress is built on a resilient, sustainable foundation. EDPR NA is proud to work with Meta as we deliver domestic power and advanced infrastructure that reinforce American energy independence and expand economic opportunity nationwide.'

'Through our partnership with EDPR, Cypress Knee Solar will bring new generation to the Arkansas grid, creating local jobs and delivering economic benefits to the community. We're proud to expand our collaboration with EDPR,' said Amanda Yang, Head of Clean and Renewable Energy, Meta.

Cypress Knee Solar will deliver long-lasting economic value to communities in Arkansas. Once operational, the project will generate approximately $ 25 million new revenue for Chicot County, helping fund essential public services and infrastructure improvements. During construction, the project is expected to support several hundred jobs. Cypress Knee is expected to be completed by 2028.

In furtherance of its commitment to meaningful community partnership and collaboration, EDPR NA has donated more than $ 77,000 to local institutions and community-based initiatives in Chicot County, including the restoration of the Lakeside High School greenhouse, resources and supplies for the Lakeside Robotics team, and funding for the Lake Village food pantry.

About EDP Renewables North America

EDP Renewables North America LLC, its affiliates, and its subsidiaries develop, construct, own, and operate wind farms, solar parks, and energy storage systems throughout North America. Headquartered in Houston, Texas, with 61 wind farms, 29 solar parks, four energy storage sites, and eight regional offices across North America, EDPR NA has developed more than 12,900 megawatts (MW) and operates more than 12,300 MW of onshore utility-scale renewable energy projects. With more than 1,000 employees, EDPR NA's highly qualified team has a proven capacity to execute projects across the continent.

For more information, visit www.edprnorthamerica.com and follow us on LinkedIn.

[Editorial queries for this story should be sent to newswire@enpublishing.co.uk ]

value.Title: Entergy New Orleans uses drone technology to strengthen reliability

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH9-NWV3-SBT2-54KS-00000-00Entergy New Orleans uses drone technology to strengthen reliability2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:17ZLexisNexisEntergy New Orleans uses drone technology to strengthen reliabilityTargeted News ServiceNEW ORLEANS, Louisiana

[Category: BizEnergy] -- Entergy posted the following news release:

* * *

Entergy New Orleans uses drone technology to strengthen reliability

*

Entergy New Orleans is committed to enhancing grid reliability and improving the quality of service for our customers. Since 2019, the company has deployed advanced drone technology to inspect transmission lines spanning critical infrastructure and at challenging locations. At present, Entergy New Orleans is the only operating company within the enterprise utilizing this technology for inspection purposes.ÂÂ

In 2017, a lightning strike near the I-10 and I-610 interchange damaged a utility pole, resulting in live wires falling onto the interstate. This incident led to significant traffic delays and a widespread power outage, highlighting the importance of safer and more proactive ways to monitor critical infrastructure.

"Using drone technology gives us the ability to safely inspect infrastructure that can be challenging to access through traditional methods," said a Jason Goodick, manager of corporate aviation at Entergy. "It allows our engineers to review detailed imagery, identify potential issues early, and make proactive repairs that help improve reliability for our customers."

Federal regulations restrict drone operations over critical infrastructure, and industry guidelines recommend maintaining a minimum distance of 35 feet from active transmission lines. To ensure safe and compliant inspections, we employ specialized flight protocols and leverage advanced technology.

Our drones capture high-resolution video of transmission lines and equipment, along with infrared imagery that can reveal potential issues that are not visible during ground inspections. This footage is then reviewed by our engineering teams, enabling us to prioritize maintenance and address concerns before they potentially lead to outages.

Through routine inspections prior to storm season and throughout the year, we are strengthening safety protocols, minimizing service interruptions, and implementing innovative strategies to ensure the reliable maintenance of the electric grid.

Click here to watch the drone video footage.

About Entergy New Orleans

Entergy New Orleans provides electricity to more than 209,000 customers in Orleans Parish, Louisiana. Entergy New Orleans is a subsidiary of Entergy Corporation. Entergy generates, transmits and distributes electricity to power life for more than 3 million customers through our operating companies in Arkansas, Louisiana, Mississippi and Texas. We're focused on keeping costs for our customers as low as possible while providing reliable energy that our communities count on. We're also investing in growth for the future with a more resilient, cleaner energy system that includes modern natural gas, nuclear and renewable energy generation. As a nationally recognized leader in sustainability and corporate citizenship, we deliver more than $100 million in economic benefits each year to the communities we serve through philanthropy, volunteerism and advocacy. Entergy is a Fortune 500 company headquartered in New Orleans, Louisiana, and has approximately 12,000 employees. Learn more at EntergyNewOrleans.com and connect with @EntergyNOLA on social media.

AMERICAN RESULTS

OGE Energy (NYSE:OGE), announced EPS of 24.0c for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], down 29% sequentially from 34.0c in the previous quarter [Q4/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Interest expenses to Sales was down from 9.6% to 8.4%

Unfavourable Changes:

- Net profit was down 19.9% from $62.7m to $50.2m

- EPS slumped 22.6% from 31.0c to 24.0c

- EBITDA to Sales was down from 37.7% to 34.5%

- EBIT to Sales was down from 19.4% to 16.3%

- Profit before tax to Sales was down from 9.7% to 7.9%

Note:

- Total revenue was steady at $752.6m

- Depreciation to Sales was down from 18.4% to 18.1%

Sequential comparison and comparison with previous corresponding quarter:

ACTIVITIES:

OGE Energy Corp., a leading energy services holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary, Oklahoma Gas and Electric Company (OG&E), to provide reliable electric utility services. The company's core activities include electricity generation, transmission, and distribution to customers in Oklahoma and western Arkansas, with a focus on maintaining a resilient infrastructure and adhering to regulatory standards. In recent years, OGE's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar power projects, driven by increasing demand for sustainable energy and supportive policies. Additionally, investments in smart grid technologies and grid modernization efforts are accelerating, enhancing operational efficiency and enabling better integration of clean energy sources. This strategic emphasis positions OGE to capitalize on the evolving energy market while ensuring long-term value for stakeholders.

MARKET POSITION:

OGE Energy Corp. (NYSE:OGE), the NYSE's 5th largest Pipeline company by market cap, increased $3.31 (or 7.5%) in the trailing 12 months to April 29, 2026 to close at $47.34. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 29.1% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -21.5%. Average daily volume of 1.3 million shares in the trailing year was 1.1 times average daily volume in the previous year.

Past Month: Monthly: Entergy Corporation (NYSE:ETR) decreases 3% on robust volume

Entergy Corporation (NYSE:ETR $112.90), the NYSE's 4th largest Conventional Electricity company by market cap, has decreased $2.94 (or 2.5%) in the past month to close at $112.90 on Thursday May 14, ending a four-month streak of rises. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 533.9 points (or 7.7%) in the month, this represented a relative price change of -10.2%.

The volume was 1.4 times average trading of 60.2 million shares.

Activities

Entergy Corporation is a major U.S.-based energy provider listed on the New York Stock Exchange, delivering reliable electric power to over 3 million customers across Arkansas, Louisiana, Mississippi, and Texas. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity through a diversified portfolio that includes nuclear, natural gas, coal, and renewable sources. In recent years, Entergy has prioritized growth in renewable energy segments, particularly solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean power and supportive regulatory environments. These initiatives align with the company's broader strategy to enhance grid reliability, reduce carbon emissions, and integrate advanced technologies for efficient energy delivery, positioning Entergy as a key player in the evolving utility sector. It is the NYSE's 4th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Entergy Corporation (NYSE:ETR) stands as a pivotal player in the energy sector, underscoring its influence with the Entergy Texas Orange County Advanced Power Station. Achieving the Entergy Orange County first fire marks a crucial EntergyTexas first fire milestone. With the Orange County Advanced Power Station first fire accomplished, the subsequent Orange County power station commissioning progresses smoothly. This Entergy power station start-up highlights Entergy advanced power station capabilities. Noteworthy is the new Entergy power plant Orange County, vital for grid reliability EntergyTexas. As Entergy Texas power generation capacity expands, the first fire milestone power plant enhances Entergy utility infrastructure. Through Entergy plant commissioning timeline and operational readiness testing, commercial operation Entergy advances. These Entergy energy infrastructure projects epitomize industrial power solutions Entergy. Power station milestone news Entergy underscores Entergy NYSE:ETR power projects while bolstering Entergy Texas energy reliability. These initiatives bolster Entergy community energy projects and Entergy electricity supply solutions, fortifying their market position.

Past Month: Monthly: Deckers Outdoor Corporation (NYSE:DECK) slides 12% on weak volume

Deckers Outdoor Corporation (NYSE:DECK $94.97), the NYSE's 9th largest Consumer Cyclical company by market cap, has slid $12.89 (or 12.0%) in the past month to close at $94.97 on Thursday May 14. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 533.9 points (or 7.7%) in the month, this represented a relative price change of -19.6%.

The volume was 0.6 times average trading of 65.6 million shares.

Activities

Deckers Outdoor Corporation, listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: DECK), is a global leader in designing, marketing, and distributing innovative footwear, apparel, and accessories. The company's portfolio includes popular brands such as UGG, Hoka, Teva, and Sanuk, focusing on casual, outdoor, and performance products that emphasize comfort, quality, and style. Core activities involve product development, global distribution through wholesale and direct-to-consumer channels, and strategic brand management to drive consumer engagement. In recent years, the fastest-growing segments have been Hoka, which has surged due to rising demand for high-performance running and athletic footwear, and UGG's expansion into lifestyle apparel. This growth has bolstered Deckers' revenue, enabling investments in sustainability and digital initiatives to strengthen its market position in the competitive outdoor and athletic goods sector. It is the NYSE's 7th largest Consumer cyclical company by market capitalisation.

Deckers Outdoor Corporation (NYSE:DECK) is a global leader in designing, marketing, and distributing innovative footwear, with a diverse portfolio that includes UGG, HOKA, Teva, and Sanuk. Deckers excels in creative collaborations, exemplified by the UGG x PinkPantheress collection, featuring UGG PinkPantheress boots and the UGG limited edition PinkPantheress line. The UGG Classic Mini PinkPantheress and UGG platform slippers are at the forefront of UGG festival footwear, appealing to style-conscious consumers. In addition, Deckers’ HOKA running shoes, such as the HOKA Clifton running shoes and HOKA Bondi sneakers, are renowned for their superior performance and comfort. Teva festival sandals and Sanuk casual sandals further diversify its product offerings. With a robust supply chain and strong brand recognition, Deckers is strategically positioned for sustained growth and resilience in the competitive footwear market, capitalizing on consumer trends and UGG celebrity collaboration collection initiatives.

Past Month: Monthly: Entergy Corporation (NYSE:ETR) decreases 3% on robust volume

Entergy Corporation (NYSE:ETR $112.93), the NYSE's 4th largest Conventional Electricity company by market cap, has decreased $2.91 (or 2.5%) in the past month to close at $112.93 on Tuesday May 12, ending a three-month streak of rises. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 584.1 points (or 8.6%) in the month, this represented a relative price change of -11.1%.

The volume was 1.4 times average trading of 60.4 million shares.

Activities

Entergy Corporation is a major U.S.-based energy provider listed on the New York Stock Exchange, delivering reliable electric power to over 3 million customers across Arkansas, Louisiana, Mississippi, and Texas. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity through a diversified portfolio that includes nuclear, natural gas, coal, and renewable sources. In recent years, Entergy has prioritized growth in renewable energy segments, particularly solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean power and supportive regulatory environments. These initiatives align with the company's broader strategy to enhance grid reliability, reduce carbon emissions, and integrate advanced technologies for efficient energy delivery, positioning Entergy as a key player in the evolving utility sector. It is the NYSE's 4th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Entergy Corporation (NYSE:ETR) stands as a pivotal player in the energy sector, underscoring its influence with the Entergy Texas Orange County Advanced Power Station. Achieving the Entergy Orange County first fire marks a crucial EntergyTexas first fire milestone. With the Orange County Advanced Power Station first fire accomplished, the subsequent Orange County power station commissioning progresses smoothly. This Entergy power station start-up highlights Entergy advanced power station capabilities. Noteworthy is the new Entergy power plant Orange County, vital for grid reliability EntergyTexas. As Entergy Texas power generation capacity expands, the first fire milestone power plant enhances Entergy utility infrastructure. Through Entergy plant commissioning timeline and operational readiness testing, commercial operation Entergy advances. These Entergy energy infrastructure projects epitomize industrial power solutions Entergy. Power station milestone news Entergy underscores Entergy NYSE:ETR power projects while bolstering Entergy Texas energy reliability. These initiatives bolster Entergy community energy projects and Entergy electricity supply solutions, fortifying their market position.

Past Month: Monthly: Deckers Outdoor Corporation (NYSE:DECK) sinks 12% on weak volume

Deckers Outdoor Corporation (NYSE:DECK $94.89), the NYSE's 9th largest Consumer Cyclical company by market cap, has sunk $12.97 (or 12.0%) in the past month to close at $94.89 on Tuesday May 12. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 584.1 points (or 8.6%) in the month, this represented a relative price change of -20.6%.

The volume was 0.6 times average trading of 65.7 million shares.

Activities

Deckers Outdoor Corporation, listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: DECK), is a global leader in designing, marketing, and distributing innovative footwear, apparel, and accessories. The company's portfolio includes popular brands such as UGG, Hoka, Teva, and Sanuk, focusing on casual, outdoor, and performance products that emphasize comfort, quality, and style. Core activities involve product development, global distribution through wholesale and direct-to-consumer channels, and strategic brand management to drive consumer engagement. In recent years, the fastest-growing segments have been Hoka, which has surged due to rising demand for high-performance running and athletic footwear, and UGG's expansion into lifestyle apparel. This growth has bolstered Deckers' revenue, enabling investments in sustainability and digital initiatives to strengthen its market position in the competitive outdoor and athletic goods sector. It is the NYSE's 7th largest Consumer cyclical company by market capitalisation.

Deckers Outdoor Corporation (NYSE:DECK) is a global leader in designing, marketing, and distributing innovative footwear, with a diverse portfolio that includes UGG, HOKA, Teva, and Sanuk. Deckers excels in creative collaborations, exemplified by the UGG x PinkPantheress collection, featuring UGG PinkPantheress boots and the UGG limited edition PinkPantheress line. The UGG Classic Mini PinkPantheress and UGG platform slippers are at the forefront of UGG festival footwear, appealing to style-conscious consumers. In addition, Deckers’ HOKA running shoes, such as the HOKA Clifton running shoes and HOKA Bondi sneakers, are renowned for their superior performance and comfort. Teva festival sandals and Sanuk casual sandals further diversify its product offerings. With a robust supply chain and strong brand recognition, Deckers is strategically positioned for sustained growth and resilience in the competitive footwear market, capitalizing on consumer trends and UGG celebrity collaboration collection initiatives.

Past Month: Monthly: M&T Bank Corporation (NYSE:MTB) drops 5% on average volume

M&T Bank Corporation (NYSE:MTB $208.75), the NYSE's 13th largest Bank by market cap, has dropped $11.17 (or 5.1%) in the past month to close at $208.75 on Tuesday May 12. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 584.1 points (or 8.6%) in the month, this represented a relative price change of -13.7%.

The volume was 0.9 times average trading of 24.4 million shares.

Activities

M&T Bank Corporation is a prominent regional bank holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, headquartered in Buffalo, New York, and operating primarily in the Mid-Atlantic and Northeastern United States. Its core business activities include commercial banking, retail banking, and wealth management services, such as lending, deposit products, and investment advisory for individuals, businesses, and institutions. The bank has seen robust growth in its commercial lending and digital banking segments, fueled by strategic acquisitions like the 2022 merger with People's United Bank, which expanded its footprint and enhanced technological capabilities. These areas have driven increased revenue from business loans and online services, supporting overall profitability and market expansion in a competitive financial sector. M&T Bank's focus on innovation and customer-centric strategies positions it as a reliable partner for diverse financial needs. It is the NYSE's 9th largest Bank by market capitalisation.

M&T Bank Corporation (NYSE:MTB) is strategically positioned in the financial industry as a prominent regional bank. The company recently announced an initiative encapsulated by the M&T Bank subordinated notes offering. As part of this strategy, the M&T Bank $500 million subordinated notes issuance signifies a targeted approach towards strengthening its financial position. This MTB subordinated notes offering is a pivotal M&T Bank subordinated debt offering that reflects its commitment to maintaining capital adequacy and supporting growth. The M&T Bank notes offering reported by Investing.com highlights the M&T Bank $500M debt issuance details. The NYSE:MTB subordinated notes are a key component of the M&T Bank bond offering, ensuring robust participation in the M&T Bank debt securities offering and expanding its investor base. Furthermore, M&T Bank's fixed-income offering, including the M&T Bank capital raise subordinated notes, emphasizes its M&T Bank corporate subordinated bonds drive. As noted in the M&T Bank private placement subordinated notes and Investing.com M&T Bank subordinated notes, the M&T Bank investor news subordinated notes strategy underscores the significance of the MTB $500M offering in ongoing M&T Bank corporate debt issuance efforts. Deere& Company (NYSE:DE $588.97), the NYSE's largest Industrial company by market cap, has decreased $16.03 (or 2.6%) in the past month to close at $588.97 on Tuesday May 12, ending a four-month streak of rises. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 584.1 points (or 8.6%) in the month, this represented a relative price change of -11.2%.

The volume was 0.7 times average trading of 28.7 million shares.

Activities

Deere & Company, a prominent American manufacturer listed on the New York Stock Exchange, specializes in the design, production, and distribution of agricultural, construction, and forestry equipment. As a global leader in the industry, the company focuses on innovative machinery that enhances productivity and efficiency, including tractors, combines, excavators, and loaders. Its core activities encompass manufacturing high-quality equipment, providing financial services for equipment financing, and offering digital solutions to support modern farming and construction operations. Among its fastest-growing segments are precision agriculture technologies, such as autonomous tractors and data analytics tools, which leverage AI and IoT for optimized resource use, as well as the construction equipment division, driven by increasing global infrastructure projects and demand for sustainable machinery. This strategic emphasis on innovation and expansion positions Deere to meet evolving market needs while maintaining a strong commitment to environmental stewardship. It is the NYSE's largest Industrial company by market capitalisation.

Deere & Company (NYSE:DE) positions itself as an industry leader through its commitment to sustainability and innovation. The Deere & Company recycled plastics initiative demonstrates its efforts towards a circular economy agriculture model. John Deere tractors recycled plastics extend through the use of John Deere composite plastic parts, while John Deere recycled ABS parts signify advancements in material usage. In line with sustainable materials R&D, John Deere plastic recycling in equipment manufacturing is pivotal. Products like John Deere recycled plastic seats, John Deere recycled plastic mower decks, and John Deere recycled plastic cab interiors act as examples of eco-friendly agricultural implements. John Deere plastic component durability testing ensures the longevity of these innovations. Moreover, collaborations with John Deere sustainable parts suppliers enhance the availability of John Deere biodegradable plastic alternatives. John Deere eco-friendly tractors and John Deere green farming equipment are testaments to Deere & Company's leadership in the sustainable farm equipment domain. John Deere tests recycled plastics for quality assurance, manifesting its prowess in recycled plastics prototypes.

Activities

Freeport-McMoRan Inc. is a leading U.S.-based mining company specializing in the exploration, development, and production of copper, gold, and molybdenum. Its core business focuses on sustainable resource management, emphasizing operational efficiency and environmental stewardship to meet rising demand. The fastest-growing segments include copper production, which has expanded rapidly due to increasing needs in electric vehicles and infrastructure projects, as well as gold operations that have benefited from higher prices and new mine developments. Overall, Freeport-McMoRan's strategic investments in high-potential assets position it as a key player in the global metals market, driving long-term value through diversified revenue streams and innovation. With distinguished assets such as the Grasberg copper mine, Indonesia copper mines Freeport, and North American copper mines Freeport, the company plays a pivotal role in the global industrial copper supply FCX. Entergy Corporation (NYSE:ETR $117.36), the NYSE's 4th largest Conventional Electricity company by market cap, has risen $3.10 (or 2.7%) to close at $117.36 on Tuesday May 05. The stock rose for a second consecutive month on Tuesday bringing its two-month rise to $12.45 or 11.9%. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 428.5 points (or 6.3%) in the two months, this represented a relative price increase of 5.6%.

Activities

Entergy Corporation is a major U.S.-based energy provider listed on the New York Stock Exchange, delivering reliable electric power to over 3 million customers across Arkansas, Louisiana, Mississippi, and Texas. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity through a diversified portfolio that includes nuclear, natural gas, coal, and renewable sources. In recent years, Entergy has prioritized growth in renewable energy segments, particularly solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean power and supportive regulatory environments. These initiatives align with the company's broader strategy to enhance grid reliability, reduce carbon emissions, and integrate advanced technologies for efficient energy delivery, positioning Entergy as a key player in the evolving utility sector. It is the NYSE's 4th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Citizens Financial Group, Inc. (NYSE:CFG $64.36), the NYSE's 11th largest Bank by market cap, has risen $3.81 (or 6.3%) to close at $64.36 on Tuesday May 05. Activities

Citizens Financial Group, Inc., a leading U.S. bank holding company traded on the New York Stock Exchange, specializes in retail and commercial banking, wealth management, and mortgage lending services. Headquartered in Providence, Rhode Island, the company operates through its primary subsidiary, Citizens Bank, serving millions of customers across the United States with a focus on personalized financial solutions. Its core activities include deposit accounts, loans, credit cards, and investment advisory services, emphasizing digital innovation to enhance accessibility and efficiency. The fastest-growing segments are digital banking platforms, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing consumer demand for mobile and online services, and commercial lending, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises, fueled by economic recovery and strategic partnerships. With a strong commitment to community banking and sustainable practices, Citizens Financial Group continues to solidify its position as a reliable financial partner in a competitive market. It is the NYSE's 11th largest Bank by market capitalisation.

ONEOK, Inc. (NYSE:OKE $90.02), the NYSE's largest Gas Distribution company by market cap, has risen $2.78 (or 3.2%) on high volume to close at $90.02 on Tuesday May 05. The stock advanced for a sixth straight month on Tuesday bringing its a six-month rise to $25.42 or 39.4%. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 462.9 points (or 6.8%) in the six months, this represented a relative price increase of 32.5%.

The volume in the month equalled the average trading of 81.9 million shares.

Activities

ONEOK, Inc., a Fortune 500 company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading midstream energy provider in the United States, focusing on the gathering, processing, transportation, storage, and marketing of natural gas and natural gas liquids (NGLs). Its core operations span an extensive network of pipelines, processing plants, and storage facilities across major basins like the Permian and Williston, supporting the efficient movement of energy resources to end markets. The company's fastest-growing segments include NGL fractionation and logistics, fueled by surging shale production and increasing demand for refined products such as ethane and propane. This growth is further bolstered by strategic investments in infrastructure and acquisitions, enhancing ONEOK's ability to capitalize on the expanding natural gas market while maintaining operational efficiency and environmental stewardship. It is the NYSE's largest Gas distribution company by market capitalisation.

ONEOK, Inc. (NYSE:OKE) provides a robust portfolio of midstream services with a focus on ONEOK NGLs and ONEOK natural gas liquids. Strategically positioned, ONEOK stock (OKE) reflects its potential with sustainable ONEOK fee-based revenue and an impressive ONEOK dividend yield. The ONEOK capex plan supports a comprehensive ONEOK growth backlog, enhancing ONEOK pipeline capacity and ONEOK throughput volumes. Key assets include ONEOK NGL pipelines, ONEOK gas gathering, and ONEOK gas processing plants, optimizing ONEOK operational synergies. ONEOK earnings benefit from ONEOK synergies achieved through focusing on ONEOK NGL fractionation capacity, ONEOK NGL storage, and ONEOK NGL marketing, with specific emphasis on ONEOK propane, ONEOK ethane, ONEOK butane, and ONEOK condensate. With an effective ONEOK FCF forecast, ONEOK free cash flow aids ONEOK debt reduction. The ONEOK distribution coverage ratio is robust due to ONEOK growth projects backlog and ONEOK transportation services. Additionally, ONEOK merger synergies complete this strategic framework.

urn:contentItem:6JH9-K8T3-S85X-3269-00000-00Meta, EDP Renewables North America agree to contract for Chicot County solar energy EPDR partner in solar energy deal Lexicon to add line to Blytheville plant State index ends day at 1,130.35 after dip2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:17ZLexisNexisMeta, EDP Renewables North America agree to contract for Chicot County solar energy EPDR partner in solar energy deal Lexicon to add line to Blytheville plant State index ends day at 1,130.35 after dip

Meta, EPDR partner::story_summary::::default_framing::


Meta, EPDR partner
in solar energy deal
Meta, the technology company that owns Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp and Threads, and renewable energy developer EDP Renewables North America on Monday announced a long-term power purchase contract for a solar energy project in Chicot County.


Cypress Knee Solar will be a 250-megawatt solar facility in Chicot County scheduled for completion in 2028, the release said.
Cypress Knee Solar represents Meta's third energy agreement with EDP Renewables.
"Through our partnership with EDPR, Cypress Knee Solar will bring new generation to the Arkansas grid, creating local jobs and delivering economic benefits to the community," said Amanda Yang, head of Clean and Renewable Energy at Meta. "We're proud to expand our collaboration with EDPR."
EDP Renewables has donated more than $77,000 to local institutions and community-based initiatives in Chicot County, the news release said. These include the restoration of the Lakeside High School greenhouse, resources and supplies for the Lakeside Robotics team and funding for the Lake Village food pantry.
-- Sydney Sasser
Lexicon to add line
to Blytheville plant
Steel fabricator and construction company Lexicon, Inc. of Little Rock is adding an advanced robotics welding line at its Prospect Steel plant in Blytheville, the company said Monday.
The company says the first-in-the-state robotic welding line will revolutionize steel fabrication across the region and nation.
The robot, called the BLOK 500, is manufactured by Canadian robotics manufacturer AGT Robotics, which specializes in autonomous robotic welding products for the heavy fabrication sector. Lexicon's robot will fabricate large trusses and other complex structural assemblies, according to a news release.
Prospect Steel is under Lexicon's fabrication division. Prospect has two plants: the one in Blytheville, and one in Little Rock.
Lexicon's fabrication group generated $600 million in revenue for the company last year, according to a company presentation. The firm is currently undertaking a $37.6 million expansion of its Little Rock footprint.
-- Lucas Dufalla
State index ends day
at 1,130.35 after dip
The Arkansas Index, a price-weighted index that tracks the largest public companies based in the state, closed Monday at 1,130.35, down 29.37 points.
"Stocks closed lower on Monday on further confusion in the Middle East regarding control and passage through the Gulf of Hormuz, while good news on announced first quarter earnings results that doubled Wall Street expectations," said Chris Harkins, managing director at Raymond James & Associates.
The index was developed by Bloomberg News and the Arkansas Democrat-Gazette with a base value of 100 as of Dec. 30, 1997.

urn:contentItem:6JGH-8453-S3MR-41JK-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Entergy reports 63% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 30 April 2026 10:40 EDT Release Date: April 29, 20262026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:37:17ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Entergy reports 63% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 30 April 2026 10:40 EDT Release Date: April 29, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

Entergy (NYSE:ETR), announced net profit of $385m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 63% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and up 6.7% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025]. Earnings per share (EPS) were up 61.5% sequentially from 52.0c in Q4/2025 to 84.0c in Q1/2026.

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 6.7% from $360.8m to $384.9m

- Total revenue was up 12% from $2.8b to $3.2b

Unfavourable Changes:

- EBITDA to Revenues was down from 34.3% to 32%

- Profit before tax to Revenues was down from 16.2% to 15%

- EBIT to Revenues was down from 16.2% to 15%

- Profit after tax to Revenues was down from 12.7% to 12.1%

Note:

- EPS was steady at 84.0c.</dt>

- Depreciation to Revenues was down from 18% to 16.9%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

ACTIVITIES:

Entergy Corporation is a major U.S.-based energy provider listed on the New York Stock Exchange, delivering reliable electric power to over 3 million customers across Arkansas, Louisiana, Mississippi, and Texas. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity through a diversified portfolio that includes nuclear, natural gas, coal, and renewable sources. In recent years, Entergy has prioritized growth in renewable energy segments, particularly solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean power and supportive regulatory environments. These initiatives align with the company's broader strategy to enhance grid reliability, reduce carbon emissions, and integrate advanced technologies for efficient energy delivery, positioning Entergy as a key player in the evolving utility sector.

MARKET POSITION:

Entergy Corporation (NYSE:ETR), the NYSE's 4th largest Conventional Electricity company by market cap, jumped $22.84 (or 24.9%) year-to-date (YTD) in 2026 to close at $114.67. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 4.2% YTD, this is a relative price increase of 20.6%. A zero-bagger in 2025, the value of USD1,000 invested two years ago is $2,445 [vs $1,402 for the S&P 500 Index], for a capital gain of $1,293 and dividend reinvested of $152. Past Week: Weekly: OGE Energy Corp. (NYSE:OGE) ticks lower by 1.7% on firm volume

OGE Energy Corp. (NYSE:OGE $47.35), the NYSE's 6th largest Pipeline company by market cap, has ticked lower by 83c (or 1.7%) in the past week to close at $47.35.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $170.2 million to $9.7 billion.

The volume was 1.1 times average trading per week of 6.5 million shares.

Activities

OGE Energy Corp., a leading energy services holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary, Oklahoma Gas and Electric Company (OG&E), to provide reliable electric utility services. The company's core activities include electricity generation, transmission, and distribution to customers in Oklahoma and western Arkansas, with a focus on maintaining a resilient infrastructure and adhering to regulatory standards. In recent years, OGE's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar power projects, driven by increasing demand for sustainable energy and supportive policies. Additionally, investments in smart grid technologies and grid modernization efforts are accelerating, enhancing operational efficiency and enabling better integration of clean energy sources. This strategic emphasis positions OGE to capitalize on the evolving energy market while ensuring long-term value for stakeholders. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Pipeline company by market capitalisation.

OGE Energy director awarded Stock Equivalent Units recently, reflecting an integral element of OGE Energy director compensation. The OGE Energy stock equivalent units result in an OGE Energy stock award that aligns with strategic goals and shareholder interests. Through OGE Energy Corp stock grant operations, directors receive an OGE Energy equity award, further supplemented by OGE Energy restricted stock units and OGE Energy deferred stock units. The announcement of OGE Energy insider awards is detailed in the OGE Energy Form 4 filing, representation of transparent OGE Energy corporate governance stock awards.

Further insights into OGE Energy board director stock grant and OGE Energy beneficial ownership are revealed in the OGE Energy proxy statement stock awards. The OGE Energy executive compensation framework underpins director RSU award and adheres to company values, outlined in the OGE Energy shareholder disclosure and OGE Energy SEC filing equity grant documents. OGE Energy insider trading disclosure ensures transparency across OGE Energy board compensation, aligning with long-term plans and OGE Energy equity compensation plan commitments.

Past Week: OGE Energy Corp. (NYSE:OGE) shares post worst week in twenty-two weeks

OGE Energy Corp. (NYSE:OGE $47.33), the NYSE's 4th largest Pipeline company by market cap, has decreased $1.47 (or 3.0%) in the past week to close at $47.33.

The shares had their worst week in twenty-two weeks since the week-ended Dec 11, 2025.

In the past week the market cap has declined by $301.4 million to $9.7 billion.

Activities

OGE Energy Corp., a leading energy services holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary, Oklahoma Gas and Electric Company (OG&E), to provide reliable electric utility services. The company's core activities include electricity generation, transmission, and distribution to customers in Oklahoma and western Arkansas, with a focus on maintaining a resilient infrastructure and adhering to regulatory standards. In recent years, OGE's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar power projects, driven by increasing demand for sustainable energy and supportive policies. Additionally, investments in smart grid technologies and grid modernization efforts are accelerating, enhancing operational efficiency and enabling better integration of clean energy sources. This strategic emphasis positions OGE to capitalize on the evolving energy market while ensuring long-term value for stakeholders. It is the NYSE's 4th largest Pipeline company by market capitalisation.

OGE Energy director awarded Stock Equivalent Units recently, reflecting an integral element of OGE Energy director compensation. The OGE Energy stock equivalent units result in an OGE Energy stock award that aligns with strategic goals and shareholder interests. Through OGE Energy Corp stock grant operations, directors receive an OGE Energy equity award, further supplemented by OGE Energy restricted stock units and OGE Energy deferred stock units. The announcement of OGE Energy insider awards is detailed in the OGE Energy Form 4 filing, representation of transparent OGE Energy corporate governance stock awards.

Further insights into OGE Energy board director stock grant and OGE Energy beneficial ownership are revealed in the OGE Energy proxy statement stock awards. The OGE Energy executive compensation framework underpins director RSU award and adheres to company values, outlined in the OGE Energy shareholder disclosure and OGE Energy SEC filing equity grant documents. OGE Energy insider trading disclosure ensures transparency across OGE Energy board compensation, aligning with long-term plans and OGE Energy equity compensation plan commitments.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

OGE Energy Corp., a leading energy services holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary, Oklahoma Gas and Electric Company (OG&E), to provide reliable electric utility services. The company's core activities include electricity generation, transmission, and distribution to customers in Oklahoma and western Arkansas, with a focus on maintaining a resilient infrastructure and adhering to regulatory standards. In recent years, OGE's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar power projects, driven by increasing demand for sustainable energy and supportive policies. Additionally, investments in smart grid technologies and grid modernization efforts are accelerating, enhancing operational efficiency and enabling better integration of clean energy sources. This strategic emphasis positions OGE to capitalize on the evolving energy market while ensuring long-term value for stakeholders. It is the NYSE's 7th largest Pipeline company by market capitalisation.

OGE Energy director awarded Stock Equivalent Units recently, reflecting an integral element of OGE Energy director compensation. The OGE Energy stock equivalent units result in an OGE Energy stock award that aligns with strategic goals and shareholder interests. Through OGE Energy Corp stock grant operations, directors receive an OGE Energy equity award, further supplemented by OGE Energy restricted stock units and OGE Energy deferred stock units. The announcement of OGE Energy insider awards is detailed in the OGE Energy Form 4 filing, representation of transparent OGE Energy corporate governance stock awards.

Further insights into OGE Energy board director stock grant and OGE Energy beneficial ownership are revealed in the OGE Energy proxy statement stock awards. The OGE Energy executive compensation framework underpins director RSU award and adheres to company values, outlined in the OGE Energy shareholder disclosure and OGE Energy SEC filing equity grant documents. OGE Energy insider trading disclosure ensures transparency across OGE Energy board compensation, aligning with long-term plans and OGE Energy equity compensation plan commitments.

OGE Energy forecasts earnings for OG&E of approximately $533 million, or $2.57 per average diluted share; OGE Energy forecasts a loss of approximately $30 million for other operations (primarily the holding company), or a loss of $0.14 per average diluted share; OG&E experiences normal weather patterns for the year; OG&E has significant seasonality in its earnings; OG&E typically shows minimal earnings in the first and fourth quarters with a majority of its earnings in the third quarter due to the seasonal nature of air conditioning demand; total retail load growth of approximately 4 to 6 percent; operating expenses of approximately $1.228 billion to $1.238 billion, with operation and maintenance expenses comprising approximately 45 percent of the total; net interest expense of approximately $256 million to $261 million which assumes a $14 million allowance for borrowed funds used during construction reduction to interest expense, and assumes a debt issuance at OG&E of approximately $300 million; other income of approximately $24 million including $26 million of allowance for equity funds used during construction; an effective consolidated tax rate of approximately 16.8 percent; and approximately 207.3 million average diluted shares outstanding.

December 31 2025: OGE Energy: File SEC Form 10-K - Management's Discussion and Analysis 10-K

Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations.

The following combined discussion is separately filed by OGE Energy and OG&E. However, OG&E does not make any representations as to information related solely to OGE Energy or the subsidiaries of OGE Energy other than itself.

Overview

OGE Energy is a holding company whose primary investment provides electricity in Oklahoma and western Arkansas. OGE Energy's electric company operations are conducted through its wholly-owned subsidiary, OG&E, which generates, transmits, distributes and sells electric energy in Oklahoma and western Arkansas and are reported through OGE Energy's electric company business segment. OG&E's rates are subject to regulation by the OCC, the APSC and the FERC. OG&E was incorporated in 1902 under the laws of the Oklahoma Territory and is the largest electric company in Oklahoma, with a franchised service territory that includes Fort Smith, Arkansas and the surrounding communities. OG&E sold its retail natural gas business in 1928 and is no longer engaged in the natural gas distribution business.

The accounts of OGE Energy and its wholly-owned subsidiaries, including OG&E, are included in OGE Energy's consolidated financial statements. All intercompany transactions and balances are eliminated in such consolidation.

Recent Developments

OG&E's Regulatory Matters

On March 27, 2025, the OCC issued a final order approving the settlement agreement relating to OG&E's 2023 general rate review and approved the ALJ report on the remaining one MW issue with one exception. OG&E also issued its 2025 IRP to the OCC and APSC and has filed for and/or received preapproval, in both Oklahoma and Arkansas, of certain generation and capacity investments identified by OG&E's IRP and related request for proposals process.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

OGE Energy Corp., a leading energy services holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary, Oklahoma Gas and Electric Company (OG&E), to provide reliable electric utility services. The company's core activities include electricity generation, transmission, and distribution to customers in Oklahoma and western Arkansas, with a focus on maintaining a resilient infrastructure and adhering to regulatory standards. In recent years, OGE's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar power projects, driven by increasing demand for sustainable energy and supportive policies. Additionally, investments in smart grid technologies and grid modernization efforts are accelerating, enhancing operational efficiency and enabling better integration of clean energy sources. This strategic emphasis positions OGE to capitalize on the evolving energy market while ensuring long-term value for stakeholders. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Pipeline company by market capitalisation.

OGE Energy director awarded Stock Equivalent Units recently, reflecting an integral element of OGE Energy director compensation. The OGE Energy stock equivalent units result in an OGE Energy stock award that aligns with strategic goals and shareholder interests. Through OGE Energy Corp stock grant operations, directors receive an OGE Energy equity award, further supplemented by OGE Energy restricted stock units and OGE Energy deferred stock units. The announcement of OGE Energy insider awards is detailed in the OGE Energy Form 4 filing, representation of transparent OGE Energy corporate governance stock awards.

Further insights into OGE Energy board director stock grant and OGE Energy beneficial ownership are revealed in the OGE Energy proxy statement stock awards. The OGE Energy executive compensation framework underpins director RSU award and adheres to company values, outlined in the OGE Energy shareholder disclosure and OGE Energy SEC filing equity grant documents. OGE Energy insider trading disclosure ensures transparency across OGE Energy board compensation, aligning with long-term plans and OGE Energy equity compensation plan commitments.

SECTION 3 PAST YEAR PRESS RELEASES

3.1 Press Releases and Corporate Wire

Press Release article 1 of 5, 253 words

March 13: OGE Energy : Outlook

2026 Outlook

Key assumptions for the 2026 outlook are discussed below.

OGE Energy is projected to earn approximately $494 million to $514 million, or $2.38 to $2.48 per average diluted share, with a midpoint of approximately $504 million, or $2.43 per average diluted share in 2026 and is based off the following assumptions:

OGE Energy forecasts earnings for OG&E of approximately $533 million, or $2.57 per average diluted share; OGE Energy forecasts a loss of approximately $30 million for other operations (primarily the holding company), or a loss of $0.14 per average diluted share; OG&E experiences normal weather patterns for the year; OG&E has significant seasonality in its earnings; OG&E typically shows minimal earnings in the first and fourth quarters with a majority of its earnings in the third quarter due to the seasonal nature of air conditioning demand; total retail load growth of approximately 4 to 6 percent; operating expenses of approximately $1.228 billion to $1.238 billion, with operation and maintenance expenses comprising approximately 45 percent of the total; net interest expense of approximately $256 million to $261 million which assumes a $14 million allowance for borrowed funds used during construction reduction to interest expense, and assumes a debt issuance at OG&E of approximately $300 million; other income of approximately $24 million including $26 million of allowance for equity funds used during construction; an effective consolidated tax rate of approximately 16.8 percent; and approximately 207.3 million average diluted shares outstanding.

Press Release article 4 of 5, 292 words

December 31 2025: OGE Energy: File SEC Form 10-K - Management's Discussion and Analysis 10-K

Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations.

The following combined discussion is separately filed by OGE Energy and OG&E. However, OG&E does not make any representations as to information related solely to OGE Energy or the subsidiaries of OGE Energy other than itself.

Overview

OGE Energy is a holding company whose primary investment provides electricity in Oklahoma and western Arkansas. OGE Energy's electric company operations are conducted through its wholly-owned subsidiary, OG&E, which generates, transmits, distributes and sells electric energy in Oklahoma and western Arkansas and are reported through OGE Energy's electric company business segment. OG&E's rates are subject to regulation by the OCC, the APSC and the FERC. OG&E was incorporated in 1902 under the laws of the Oklahoma Territory and is the largest electric company in Oklahoma, with a franchised service territory that includes Fort Smith, Arkansas and the surrounding communities. OG&E sold its retail natural gas business in 1928 and is no longer engaged in the natural gas distribution business.

The accounts of OGE Energy and its wholly-owned subsidiaries, including OG&E, are included in OGE Energy's consolidated financial statements. All intercompany transactions and balances are eliminated in such consolidation.

Recent Developments

OG&E's Regulatory Matters

On March 27, 2025, the OCC issued a final order approving the settlement agreement relating to OG&E's 2023 general rate review and approved the ALJ report on the remaining one MW issue with one exception. OG&E also issued its 2025 IRP to the OCC and APSC and has filed for and/or received preapproval, in both Oklahoma and Arkansas, of certain generation and capacity investments identified by OG&E's IRP and related request for proposals process.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

OGE Energy Corp., a leading energy services holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary, Oklahoma Gas and Electric Company (OG&E), to provide reliable electric utility services. The company's core activities include electricity generation, transmission, and distribution to customers in Oklahoma and western Arkansas, with a focus on maintaining a resilient infrastructure and adhering to regulatory standards. In recent years, OGE's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar power projects, driven by increasing demand for sustainable energy and supportive policies. Additionally, investments in smart grid technologies and grid modernization efforts are accelerating, enhancing operational efficiency and enabling better integration of clean energy sources. This strategic emphasis positions OGE to capitalize on the evolving energy market while ensuring long-term value for stakeholders. It is the NYSE's 5th largest Pipeline company by market capitalisation.

OGE Energy director awarded Stock Equivalent Units recently, reflecting an integral element of OGE Energy director compensation. The OGE Energy stock equivalent units result in an OGE Energy stock award that aligns with strategic goals and shareholder interests. Through OGE Energy Corp stock grant operations, directors receive an OGE Energy equity award, further supplemented by OGE Energy restricted stock units and OGE Energy deferred stock units. The announcement of OGE Energy insider awards is detailed in the OGE Energy Form 4 filing, representation of transparent OGE Energy corporate governance stock awards.

Further insights into OGE Energy board director stock grant and OGE Energy beneficial ownership are revealed in the OGE Energy proxy statement stock awards. The OGE Energy executive compensation framework underpins director RSU award and adheres to company values, outlined in the OGE Energy shareholder disclosure and OGE Energy SEC filing equity grant documents. OGE Energy insider trading disclosure ensures transparency across OGE Energy board compensation, aligning with long-term plans and OGE Energy equity compensation plan commitments.

March 13: OGE Energy : Outlook

2026 Outlook

Key assumptions for the 2026 outlook are discussed below.

OGE Energy is projected to earn approximately $494 million to $514 million, or $2.38 to $2.48 per average diluted share, with a midpoint of approximately $504 million, or $2.43 per average diluted share in 2026 and is based off the following assumptions:

OGE Energy forecasts earnings for OG&E of approximately $533 million, or $2.57 per average diluted share; OGE Energy forecasts a loss of approximately $30 million for other operations (primarily the holding company), or a loss of $0.14 per average diluted share; OG&E experiences normal weather patterns for the year; OG&E has significant seasonality in its earnings; OG&E typically shows minimal earnings in the first and fourth quarters with a majority of its earnings in the third quarter due to the seasonal nature of air conditioning demand; total retail load growth of approximately 4 to 6 percent; operating expenses of approximately $1.228 billion to $1.238 billion, with operation and maintenance expenses comprising approximately 45 percent of the total; net interest expense of approximately $256 million to $261 million which assumes a $14 million allowance for borrowed funds used during construction reduction to interest expense, and assumes a debt issuance at OG&E of approximately $300 million; other income of approximately $24 million including $26 million of allowance for equity funds used during construction; an effective consolidated tax rate of approximately 16.8 percent; and approximately 207.3 million average diluted shares outstanding.

December 31 2025: OGE Energy: File SEC Form 10-K - Management's Discussion and Analysis 10-K

Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations.

The following combined discussion is separately filed by OGE Energy and OG&E. However, OG&E does not make any representations as to information related solely to OGE Energy or the subsidiaries of OGE Energy other than itself.

Overview

OGE Energy is a holding company whose primary investment provides electricity in Oklahoma and western Arkansas. OGE Energy's electric company operations are conducted through its wholly-owned subsidiary, OG&E, which generates, transmits, distributes and sells electric energy in Oklahoma and western Arkansas and are reported through OGE Energy's electric company business segment. OG&E's rates are subject to regulation by the OCC, the APSC and the FERC. OG&E was incorporated in 1902 under the laws of the Oklahoma Territory and is the largest electric company in Oklahoma, with a franchised service territory that includes Fort Smith, Arkansas and the surrounding communities. OG&E sold its retail natural gas business in 1928 and is no longer engaged in the natural gas distribution business.

The accounts of OGE Energy and its wholly-owned subsidiaries, including OG&E, are included in OGE Energy's consolidated financial statements. All intercompany transactions and balances are eliminated in such consolidation.

Recent Developments

OG&E's Regulatory Matters

On March 27, 2025, the OCC issued a final order approving the settlement agreement relating to OG&E's 2023 general rate review and approved the ALJ report on the remaining one MW issue with one exception. OG&E also issued its 2025 IRP to the OCC and APSC and has filed for and/or received preapproval, in both Oklahoma and Arkansas, of certain generation and capacity investments identified by OG&E's IRP and related request for proposals process.
